ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stores" (
  "store_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each store */,
  "store_name" TEXT /* Name of the store */,
  "qty" INTEGER /* Quantity of products in stock */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the store */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the store is located */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the store */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the store */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the store */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the store */,
  "store_manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the store manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "unit_price" TEXT /* Price of the product per unit */,
  "product_category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "store_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the store */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the product */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in stock */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last inventory update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("store_id", "product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_movements" (
  "stock_movement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock movement */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the stock movement */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the store */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the product */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the stock movement */,
  "movement_type" TEXT /* Type of the stock movement (IN or OUT) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stock_movement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "low_stock_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each low-stock alert */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the store */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the product */,
  "quantity_threshold" INTEGER /* Quantity threshold for the low-stock alert */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the low-stock alert notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)
column_names: City where the store is located
Unique identifier of the store manager
Price of the product per unit
Quantity of the product in stock
Timestamp of the last inventory update
Unique identifier for each stock movement
Type of the stock movement (IN or OUT)
Unique identifier for each low-stock alert
Quantity threshold for the low-stock alert
Status of the low-stock alert notification
